popularStory.description: Lady Agnes Stewart (died 1557) was a Scottish noble. She was born the illegitimate[1] daughter of James Stewart, 1st Earl of Buchan and Margaret Murray.[1] On 31 October 1552 she was legitimized, conf …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Stewart於1480年11月28日出生在蘇格蘭皮布萊爾特拉華爾時，她的父親James Stewart 1st Earl of Buchan38歲，母親Margaret Murray38歲。她於1511年8月28日與Adam Hepburn 2nd Earl of Bothwell, Lord Hailes在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安霍利盧德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1557年2月20日在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安享年76歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Stewart于1480年11月28日出生在苏格兰皮布萊爾特拉華爾时，她的父亲James Stewart 1st Earl of Buchan38岁，母亲Margaret Murray38岁。她于1511年8月28日与Adam Hepburn 2nd Earl of Bothwell, Lord Hailes在苏格兰米德洛迪安霍利盧德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1557年2月20日在苏格兰米德洛迪安享年76岁，埋葬在苏格兰米德洛迪安。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Stewart was born on 28 November 1480, in Traquair, Peeblesshire, Scotland, her father, James Stewart 1st Earl of Buchan, was 38 and her mother, Margaret Murray, was 38. She married Adam Hepburn 2nd Earl of Bothwell, Lord Hailes on 28 August 1511, in Holyrood, Midlothian, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 20 February 1557, in Midlothian, Scotland, at the age of 76, and was buried in Midlothian, Scotland.

fr.Traquair, Peebles-shire, Scotland: Traquair, Valle de Twede, Écosse, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Stewart est née le 28 novembre 1480, à Traquair, Valle de Twede, Écosse, son père, James Stewart 1st Earl of Buchan, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Margaret Murray, avait 38 ans. Elle a épousé Adam Hepburn 2nd Earl of Bothwell, Lord Hailes le 28 août 1511, à Holyrood, Midlothian, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 20 février 1557, à Midlothian, Écosse, à l’âge de 76 ans, et a été enterrée à Midlothian, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes Stewart am 28. November 1480 in Traquair, Peeblesshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater James Stewart 1st Earl of Buchan 38 und ihre Mutter Margaret Murray 38. Sie hat am 28. August 1511 in Holyrood, Midlothian, Scotland Adam Hepburn 2nd Earl of Bothwell, Lord Hailes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 20. Februar 1557 im Alter von 76 Jahren in Midlothian, Scotland und wurde in Midlothian, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Stewart è nata il il 28 novembre 1480 nel Traquair, Peeblesshire, Scotland, sua padre, James Stewart 1st Earl of Buchan, aveva 38 anni e sua madre, Margaret Murray, aveva 38 anni. Lei sposato Adam Hepburn 2nd Earl of Bothwell, Lord Hailes il 28 agosto 1511, nel Holyrood, Midlothian, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 20 febbraio 1557, nel Midlothian, Scotland, all'età di 76 anni, e fu sepolta a Midlothian, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Stewartが1480年11月28日にスコットランドペエブレシュアイアトラクアで生まれた時，父James Stewart 1st Earl of Buchanは38歳，母Margaret Murrayは38歳であった。1511年8月28日にスコットランドミドロスアンホーリルーッドでAdam Hepburn 2nd Earl of Bothwell, Lord Hailesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1557年2月20日にスコットランドミドロスアンで76歳で死去し，スコットランドミドロスアンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Stewart(이)가 1480년 11월 28일 스컷랜드 피블레실르 트라퀄어에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 James Stewart 1st Earl of Buchan(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Murray(은)는 38세였다. 그녀는 1511년 8월 28일 스컷랜드 미들로스이언 홀이루드에서 Adam Hepburn 2nd Earl of Bothwell, Lord Hailes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1557년 2월 20일 스컷랜드 미들로스이언에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 미들로스이언에 묻혔다.

pt.28 November 1480–20 February 1557: 28 de novembro de 1480–20 de fevereiro de 1557
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Stewart nasceu em 28 de novembro de 1480, em Traquair, Peeblesshire, Scotland, seu pai, James Stewart 1st Earl of Buchan, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Murray, tinha 38 anos. Ela casou-se com Adam Hepburn 2nd Earl of Bothwell, Lord Hailes em 28 de agosto de 1511, em Holyrood, Midlothian, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 20 de fevereiro de 1557, em Midlothian, Scotland, com 76 anos, e foi sepultada em Midlothian, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Stewart родилась 28 ноября 1480 г. в местности тракуеир, пиблессхир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени James Stewart 1st Earl of Buchan было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Murray, 38. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Adam Hepburn 2nd Earl of Bothwell, Lord Hailes 28 августа 1511 г. в местности холируд, мидлотян, Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 20 февраля 1557 г. в местности мидлотян, Шотландия в возрасте 76 лет и была похоронена в местности мидлотян, Шотландия.

es.28 November 1480–20 February 1557: 28 de noviembre de 1480–20 de febrero de 1557
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes Stewart nació el 28 de noviembre de 1480, en Traquair, Peeblesshire, Scotland, su papá, James Stewart 1st Earl of Buchan, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Margaret Murray, tenía 38 años. Se casó con Adam Hepburn 2nd Earl of Bothwell, Lord Hailes el 28 de agosto de 1511, en Holyrood, Midlothian, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 20 de febrero de 1557, en Midlothian, Scotland, a la edad de 76 años, y fue sepultada en Midlothian, Scotland.
